svg.text: Beyond simplex — static and dynamic obstructions don't coincide, and the global layer compresses
Base grid p ≤ 4, k ≤ 3. 8 semantics × 129 families.
p = 4 already fixes the minimal-boundary winners.
— static and dynamic appear on different families
8 semantics × 129 families each — what lies beyond simplex transport?
The two kinds of failure appear on different structures
— two states have the same simplex transport but differ
Needs 5 edges — the cycle structure creates a "winding number" invisible to simplices
— two states look the same now but differ
Needs only 4 edges — hidden future divergence is easier to trigger than current-output divergence
These two obstructions don't coincide — they appear on structurally different families.

g.text: Reading: Broadcast stays flat. Simplex control adds no global structure. Global-cycle laws show static ≠ dynamic boundaries. Tetra-local laws show 4-edge obstructions.
The global layer admits explicit compression beyond raw extra tokens.

svg.text: Figure 5 · Stark: Selective Memory Theory · Global Holonomy Atlas
